(auto) ensure agent ends day at home

diary:
Started the morning right—coffee in hand, checking the birdwatching forecast before heading out to Waverly Beach. The water was glassy and calm, perfect for spotting. I saw a pair of herons near the reeds and spent a good hour just watching the boats drift by, journaling. There's something about Sunday mornings that makes everything feel slower.

Grabbed groceries at QFC afterward, picked up some nice tomatoes and good coffee. Made a simple brunch at home with the fresh vegetables, wrote a letter to my sister while eating. The day was too beautiful to waste inside, so I drove over to Gas Works in the afternoon. The paddleboarders were out in force with all this sun—reminded me why I love this time of year. Grabbed coffee, watched the sailboats, let my mind wander.

Ended with an early dinner at Café Juanita, nothing fancy, just a quiet meal. Felt grounded.
thought_process: I needed this kind of day. Work's been intense lately at the restaurant, so when I saw the forecast calling for sunshine and calm waters, I made sure to carve out real time away from the city chaos. The birdwatching was deliberate—something meditative I've been neglecting. Moving between Waverly and Gas Works felt like bookending the day with water and stillness. Even the grocery stop wasn't a chore; I was intentional about it. The weather being perfect meant I could actually linger outside without guilt. By evening I was tired in a good way, ready for a quiet night. No stress, no rushing—just the kind of Sunday that reminds you why you stay in this place.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
